phase1.explanation:
Java supports multiple interface inheritance:

A class can implement ANY number of interfaces:
• class FlyingCar implements Drivable, Flyable, Swimmable

Benefits:
• Mix-and-match capabilities
• Different classes can share behaviors

Default method conflicts:
• If two interfaces have same default method
• Must OVERRIDE and resolve the conflict
• Use InterfaceName.super.methodName() to call specific interface's default

Type checking:
• instanceof works with all implemented interfaces
• Can assign to any interface type

Multiple interfaces allow for flexible design without the problems of multiple class inheritance (diamond problem).

phase3.prompt:
Create interfaces:
1. Eatable — void eat()
2. Sleepable — void sleep()
3. Playable — void play()

Create a class Pet that implements all three.
In main, create a Pet and call all three methods.
dialogueHints: Create three separate interfaces, each with one method
Call all three methods in main
phase3.openingDialogue: Why settle for one when you can have many? Multiple interfaces! 🎯
